title: Offices of Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees

communications.subject: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Offices of Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Via Email and U.S. Mail
Records Custodian
Offices of Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees
4 State Police Plaza Drive
Little Rock, AR 72209
Tel: (501) 324-9493

Re: Arkansas FOIA Request (A.C.A. § 25‑19‑101 et seq.)
    Cross County Jail and City of Wynne detention/holding facilities — inspections, staffing approvals, deficiencies, complaints, and related records (2017–present)

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act (A.C.A. § 25‑19‑101 et seq.), I request access to and electronic copies of the public records described below for the period January 1, 2017 to the present concerning:

• Cross County Detention Center/Jail (Wynne, Arkansas), and
• Any City of Wynne detention facilities or spaces (including any “city jail,” book‑in/24‑hour/overnight facilities, and temporary holding cells within the Wynne Police Department) (collectively, “Wynne PD detention/holding”).

This request is made in the public interest and relates to compliance with the Arkansas Criminal Detention Facility Standards and matters of significant community concern.

Requested Records (2017–present)

1) Inspections, findings, and transmittals (Chapter 16)
   • All onsite inspection reports, inspection checklists, findings, deficiency notices, corrective action plans, and follow‑up reports regarding Cross County Jail and Wynne PD detention/holding.
   • All transmittal letters/reports sent pursuant to § 16‑1003 (e.g., to the Chief Circuit Judge, Chief Executive, county judge, and governing body).
   • Any site visit notes, photographs, and inspector correspondence related to these facilities.
   • Any fire marshal inspection records or Department of Health food service inspection documents contained within your inspection files (see §§ 14‑1002, 12‑1003).

2) Annual staffing approvals (Chapter 9)
   • All annual staffing pattern submissions and approvals for Cross County Jail and any Wynne PD detention/holding areas, as required by § 9‑1001(A).
   • All correspondence (emails/letters/memos) between your Office/Committees and these facilities regarding staffing levels, awake/alert coverage, required visual checks, cross‑gender supervision, and emergency procedures (see §§ 9‑1001 to 9‑1002).

3) Policies reviewed or referenced in inspection files (Chs. 3, 5–15)
   For each inspection file, any copies of—or notes referencing—the following policies reviewed for Cross County Jail and Wynne PD detention/holding:
   • Intake/receiving, verification of detainment, jail logs, personal property handling, strip searches (Chapter 6; §§ 6‑1001 to 6‑1007, 6‑1013).
   • Detainee rights and disciplinary procedures (Chapter 7; §§ 7‑1001 to 7‑1003).
   • Classification and separation (Chapter 8; §§ 8‑1001 to 8‑1003).
   • Security policies: cross‑gender supervision; emergency plans for escapes/riots/fire/medical; contraband searches; key control; work‑release security (Chapter 9; § 9‑1002).
   • Medical/dental/mental health; sick call and emergency procedures; medication administration; AED/first‑aid and life‑saving certifications; detox protocols; restraints (Chapter 10; §§ 10‑1001 to 10‑1015).
   • Mail/communication/visitation (Chapter 11).
   • Food service and dietician review references (Chapter 12).
   • Safety: fire/emergency plans, training, firefighting equipment, drills (Chapter 14).
   • Detainee services/exercise where applicable (Chapter 15).
   If your Office retains only checklists or notes indicating whether required policies were produced, absent, or deficient, please provide those records.

4) New construction/remodeling plan reviews and approvals (Chapter 17)
   • All plan review submissions, narratives of compliance, coordinator comments, and Committee approval letters for any new construction or remodeling at Cross County Jail or Wynne PD detention/holding areas since 2017 (see §§ 17‑1002 to 17‑1004).
   • Any deviations/variances requested and related correspondence.

5) Temporary holding/Book‑In/Overnight facilities oversight (Chapter 18)
   • Records identifying whether Wynne PD operates a Book‑In facility, Twenty‑Four Hour/Overnight facility, or Holding Cells as defined in Chapter 18.
   • Any inspections, review materials, deficiency notices, or corrective actions specific to such units, including compliance with § 18‑1003 (toilet access, staff observation, and food/water for detentions exceeding four hours).

6) Records retention and documentation notes (Chapter 6)
   • Any inspection findings or notes concerning recordkeeping compliance, including jail logs (§ 6‑1003), critical incident documentation (§ 6‑1012), medical/dental treatment records (§§ 10‑1004, 10‑1014), detainee disciplinary files (§ 6‑1011), personal property receipts (§ 6‑1005), and strip search documentation (§ 6‑1007).

7) Complaints, inquiries, and referrals
   • All complaints, tips, or inquiries received by your Office/Committees regarding Cross County Jail and Wynne PD detention/holding (2017–present), whether from detainees, family members, attorneys, courts, or the public.
   • Any resulting investigative notes, site visit notes, correspondence with the facilities, and referrals to prosecutors or other authorities.

8) Committee/Coordinator records referencing these facilities
   • Meeting agendas, minutes, memoranda, or summaries of the Criminal Detention Facilities Review Committees or Coordinator that reference Cross County Jail or Wynne PD detention/holding, including entries regarding inspections, deficiencies, corrective actions, or noncompliance.

9) Wynne Police Department detention/holding areas (clarification and confirmation)
   • If the Wynne Police Department operates any detention/holding spaces (e.g., temporary holding cells, Book‑In facilities, or Twenty‑Four Hour/Overnight facilities), please provide:
     – All inspection reports, inspection checklists, findings, deficiency notices, corrective action plans, and follow‑up reports referencing Wynne PD detention/holding areas (2017–present).
     – All annual staffing pattern submissions and approvals referencing Wynne PD detention/holding areas, including cross‑gender supervision and visual check requirements (see §§ 9‑1001 to 9‑1002).
     – Any correspondence (emails/letters/memos) between your Office/Committees and Wynne PD or City of Wynne officials concerning these detention/holding areas, including classification under Chapter 18 and compliance with § 18‑1003 (toilets, observation, and food/water for detentions over four hours).
   • If your Office has no responsive records because Wynne PD does not operate such spaces, please confirm that in writing and identify any agency you believe maintains oversight records for Wynne PD detainee holding.

Production Format and Fees
• Please produce records electronically:
  – PDFs should be text‑searchable (OCR) and include any attachments.
  – Audio/video/photo files should be provided in native or standard playable formats with available metadata.
• If volume is large, please produce on a rolling basis as records are located.
• I request a fee waiver or reduction due to the considerable public interest in detention standards compliance and civil rights. Please advise in advance if anticipated copying costs exceed $50.

Withholdings and Redactions
If any records are withheld or redacted, please:
• Identify the specific AFOIA exemption(s) relied upon for each withholding.
• Provide a redaction/withholding log describing the record type, date, author/recipient (if not exempt), and general subject.
• Release all reasonably segregable non‑exempt portions as AFOIA requires.

Acknowledgment and Timing

Please acknowledge this request promptly and provide access to responsive records within the timelines set by A.C.A. § 25‑19‑105. If records are in active use or storage, please provide a good‑faith schedule for their availability. If any item is unclear or unduly burdensome, please contact me to discuss reasonable narrowing that preserves the scope and purpose of this request.

Thank you for your prompt attention. See Federal Court Case for verification of being a state resident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: Communications about page removals from CFPB website

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Communications about page removals from CFPB website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

During the week of May 18, CFPB has made two significant alterations to the content of the website. First, the website removed all public speeches, press releases, transcripts and other content preceding February 2025. In addition, the website has also recently removed all non-English content. Both of these decisions impact how CFPB provides services to the public and how certain audiences (historians, economists, etc.) might have used the CFPB website to cite past evidence.

As far as I can tell, there has been no public deliberation or announcement of either of these pending changes despite their potentially wide-ranging effects. I would like to request internal communications and discussions about both of these decisions and their implementation. This will likely be during the week of May 18 or before, so a range of May 2026 might suffice. I would expect such communications to be between the CFPB director's office (also known as the Front Office) including Russ Vought or some of his deputies, the chief counsel and CFPB, representatives of public-facing functions like Consumer Education and also the technical staff responsible for the website within the Design & Development division that is part of Technology & Innovation in Operations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacob Harris
